LICENSE AGREEMENT

This License Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of 30 October 2022 (the "Execution Date"), 
by and between Summit Bridge Capital ("Party A") and Titan Financial Services ("Party B"), collectively referred to as the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, Party A desires to engage Party B to provide intellectual property management and licensing;
WHEREAS, Party B is qualified and willing to provide such services under the terms set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, 
the Parties agree as follows:

1. EFFECTIVE DATE AND TERM

This Agreement shall become effective on 13 November 2022 (the "Effective Date") and shall 
continue in full force until 2024-05-08, unless earlier terminated in accordance with 
the provisions of this Agreement.

2. SERVICES

Party B shall provide intellectual property management and licensing as may be requested by Party A from time to time, 
subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

3. COMPENSATION

3.1 In consideration for the services rendered, Party A agrees to pay Party B the sum 
of $1,000,000 upon execution of this Agreement.

3.2 Additional compensation of $4,200,000 shall be payable upon satisfactory completion 
of each project milestone as mutually agreed upon by the Parties in writing.

3.3 Party A shall also pay a monthly retainer fee of $750,000 for ongoing advisory 
services throughout the term of this Agreement.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY

Each Party acknowledges that it may receive confidential and proprietary information 
of the other Party. Each Party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all such 
information and not to disclose it to any third party without prior written consent.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

All work product, inventions, and deliverables created by Party B in connection with 
this Agreement shall be considered work-for-hire and shall be the exclusive property 
of Party A upon full payment of all fees.

6. TERMINATION

This Agreement may be terminated immediately upon material breach by either party.

Upon termination, Party B shall promptly deliver all work product and return or 
destroy all confidential information of Party A.

7. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, 
EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, HOWEVER CAUSED, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE 
POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

8. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of 
the State of Texas, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to 
the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, 
both written and oral, between the Parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date 
first written above.

SUMMIT BRIDGE CAPITAL                    TITAN FINANCIAL SERVICES

the Parties agree as follows:

continue in full force until 03/30/2026, unless earlier terminated in accordance with 
the provisions of this Agreement.

subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

by and between Pacific Rim Capital Group ("Party A") and Northern Star Enterprises ("Party B"), collectively referred to as the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, Party A desires to engage Party B to provide legal advisory and document review services;
WHEREAS, Party B is qualified and willing to provide such services under the terms set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, 
the Parties agree as follows:

1. EFFECTIVE DATE AND TERM

This Agreement shall become effective on 2023-01-25 (the "Effective Date") and shall 
continue in full force until March 04, 2024, unless earlier terminated in accordance with 
the provisions of this Agreement.

2. SERVICES

Party B shall provide legal advisory and document review services as may be requested by Party A from time to time, 
subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

3. COMPENSATION

3.1 In consideration for the services rendered, Party A agrees to pay Party B the sum 
of $1,500,000 upon execution of this Agreement.

3.2 Additional compensation of $2,500,000 shall be payable upon satisfactory completion 
of each project milestone as mutually agreed upon by the Parties in writing.

3.3 Party A shall also pay a monthly retainer fee of $300,000 for ongoing advisory 
services throughout the term of this Agreement.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY

Each Party acknowledges that it may receive confidential and proprietary information 
of the other Party. Each Party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all such 
information and not to disclose it to any third party without prior written consent.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

All work product, inventions, and deliverables created by Party B in connection with 
this Agreement shall be considered work-for-hire and shall be the exclusive property 
of Party A upon full payment of all fees.

6. TERMINATION

Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice.

Upon termination, Party B shall promptly deliver all work product and return or 
destroy all confidential information of Party A.

7. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, 
EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, HOWEVER CAUSED, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE 
POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

8. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of 
the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to 
the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, 
both written and oral, between the Parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date 
first written above.

MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of 2023-12-11 (the "Execution Date"), 
by and between Summit Bridge Capital ("Party A") and Vantage Point Holdings LLC ("Party B"), collectively referred to as the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, Party A desires to engage Party B to provide financial consulting and portfolio management;
WHEREAS, Party B is qualified and willing to provide such services under the terms set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, 
the Parties agree as follows:

1. EFFECTIVE DATE AND TERM

This Agreement shall become effective on December 13, 2023 (the "Effective Date") and shall 
continue in full force until 2025-11-11, unless earlier terminated in accordance with 
the provisions of this Agreement.

2. SERVICES

Party B shall provide financial consulting and portfolio management as may be requested by Party A from time to time, 
subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

3. COMPENSATION

3.1 In consideration for the services rendered, Party A agrees to pay Party B the sum 
of $500,000 upon execution of this Agreement.

3.2 Additional compensation of $250,000 shall be payable upon satisfactory completion 
of each project milestone as mutually agreed upon by the Parties in writing.

3.3 Party A shall also pay a monthly retainer fee of $1,500,000 for ongoing advisory 
services throughout the term of this Agreement.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY

Each Party acknowledges that it may receive confidential and proprietary information 
of the other Party. Each Party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all such 
information and not to disclose it to any third party without prior written consent.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

All work product, inventions, and deliverables created by Party B in connection with 
this Agreement shall be considered work-for-hire and shall be the exclusive property 
of Party A upon full payment of all fees.

6. TERMINATION

Either party reserves the right to terminate this Agreement for convenience upon forty-five (45) days notice.

Upon termination, Party B shall promptly deliver all work product and return or 
destroy all confidential information of Party A.

7. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, 
EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, HOWEVER CAUSED, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE 
POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

8. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of 
the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to 
the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, 
both written and oral, between the Parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date 
first written above.

SUMMIT BRIDGE CAPITAL                    VANTAGE POINT HOLDINGS LLC

By: _______________________         By: _______________________
Name: ____________________          Name: ____________________
Title: ____________________          Title: ____________________
Date: 2023-12-11               Date: 2023-12-11

by and between Global Trade Ventures Ltd. ("Party A") and Crestview Legal Services ("Party B"), collectively referred to as the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, Party A desires to engage Party B to provide data analytics and business intelligence services;
WHEREAS, Party B is qualified and willing to provide such services under the terms set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, 
the Parties agree as follows:

1. EFFECTIVE DATE AND TERM

This Agreement shall become effective on 18 November 2020 (the "Effective Date") and shall 
continue in full force until 07 May 2022, unless earlier terminated in accordance with 
the provisions of this Agreement.

2. SERVICES

Party B shall provide data analytics and business intelligence services as may be requested by Party A from time to time, 
subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

3. COMPENSATION

3.1 In consideration for the services rendered, Party A agrees to pay Party B the sum 
of $2,500,000 upon execution of this Agreement.

3.2 Additional compensation of $250,000 shall be payable upon satisfactory completion 
of each project milestone as mutually agreed upon by the Parties in writing.

3.3 Party A shall also pay a monthly retainer fee of $1,500,000 for ongoing advisory 
services throughout the term of this Agreement.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY

Each Party acknowledges that it may receive confidential and proprietary information 
of the other Party. Each Party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all such 
information and not to disclose it to any third party without prior written consent.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

All work product, inventions, and deliverables created by Party B in connection with 
this Agreement shall be considered work-for-hire and shall be the exclusive property 
of Party A upon full payment of all fees.

6. TERMINATION

This Agreement may be terminated immediately upon material breach by either party.

Upon termination, Party B shall promptly deliver all work product and return or 
destroy all confidential information of Party A.

7. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, 
EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, HOWEVER CAUSED, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE 
POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

8. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of 
the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to 
the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, 
both written and oral, between the Parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date 
first written above.

by and between Apex Capital Partners LLC ("Party A") and Crestview Legal Services ("Party B"), collectively referred to as the "Parties."

RECITALS

WHEREAS, Party A desires to engage Party B to provide mergers and acquisitions due diligence services;
WHEREAS, Party B is qualified and willing to provide such services under the terms set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, 
the Parties agree as follows:

1. EFFECTIVE DATE AND TERM

This Agreement shall become effective on 04/03/2021 (the "Effective Date") and shall 
continue in full force until 17 March 2022, unless earlier terminated in accordance with 
the provisions of this Agreement.

2. SERVICES

Party B shall provide mergers and acquisitions due diligence services as may be requested by Party A from time to time, 
subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

3. COMPENSATION

3.1 In consideration for the services rendered, Party A agrees to pay Party B the sum 
of $675,000 upon execution of this Agreement.

3.2 Additional compensation of $850,000 shall be payable upon satisfactory completion 
of each project milestone as mutually agreed upon by the Parties in writing.

3.3 Party A shall also pay a monthly retainer fee of $500,000 for ongoing advisory 
services throughout the term of this Agreement.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY

Each Party acknowledges that it may receive confidential and proprietary information 
of the other Party. Each Party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all such 
information and not to disclose it to any third party without prior written consent.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

All work product, inventions, and deliverables created by Party B in connection with 
this Agreement shall be considered work-for-hire and shall be the exclusive property 
of Party A upon full payment of all fees.

6. TERMINATION

Either party may terminate without cause upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other party.

Upon termination, Party B shall promptly deliver all work product and return or 
destroy all confidential information of Party A.

7. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, 
EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, HOWEVER CAUSED, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE 
POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

8. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of 
the State of Florida, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to 
the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, 
both written and oral, between the Parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date 
first written above.
